queries.title: Online Browsing to In-Store Purchases
queries.description: Identify customers who viewed a product online and purchased it at their preferred local physical storefront.
queries.gql:
GRAPH RetailMarketingGraph
MATCH (c:Customer)-[:Views]->(p:Product), (c)-[:PrefersStore]->(s:Store)
MATCH (c)-[purch:Purchased]->(p)
WHERE purch.store_id = s.store_id
RETURN c.name AS customer_name, p.name AS product_name, s.name AS store_name, purch.price_paid AS price_paid
queries.talk_track: This query bridges online behavioral signals (website views) with physical retail store sales. In a conventional relational database, joining these events across multiple user-journey tables involves expensive and slow multi-way joins. Spanner Graph natively traverses these multi-hop relations, delivering real-time customer analytics at global scale.

queries.title: High-Value Platinum Cart Abandonment
queries.description: Find Platinum loyalty customers who spent over 3 minutes viewing a high-value product (price > 100) but have not purchased it.
queries.gql:
GRAPH RetailMarketingGraph
MATCH (c:Customer {loyalty_tier: 'Platinum'})-[v:Views]->(p:Product)
WHERE v.duration_seconds > 180 AND CAST(p.price AS NUMERIC) > 100
AND NOT MATCH (c)-[:Purchased]->(p)
RETURN c.name AS customer_name, c.email AS customer_email, p.name AS product_name, p.price AS product_price, v.duration_seconds AS view_duration
queries.talk_track: Finding negative relationships—identifying customers who performed an action (views) but *did not* perform another (purchase)—is historically hard for SQL and highly resource-intensive. GQL's native `NOT MATCH` pattern allows us to query transactional records and generate instant high-intent target lists for email re-engagement campaigns.

queries.title: Cross-Category Product Affinity
queries.description: Discover pairs of products from different categories purchased by the same customers to drive recommendation engines.
queries.gql:
GRAPH RetailMarketingGraph
MATCH (p1:Product)<-[:Purchased]-(c:Customer)-[:Purchased]->(p2:Product)
WHERE p1.category != p2.category AND p1.product_id < p2.product_id
RETURN p1.category AS category_a, p2.category AS category_b, COUNT(c) AS customer_count
ORDER BY customer_count DESC
LIMIT 10
queries.talk_track: Understanding product affinity and co-purchasing patterns helps marketers design bundle offers and optimize site recommendations. Using Spanner Graph, we trace two purchase steps back from a shared customer node to establish a direct category correlation, avoiding the overhead of massive self-joins on transactional tables.
